rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, September 14, 2012, Line 1, DEFENDANTS STEVEN BROOKS and CAHTERINE BROOKS, BROOKS 2006 CHARITABLE REMAINDER UNITRUST DATED 3/14/2006'S Application For Order To Show Cause Re Contempt. Hearing required. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: YIN FALK VS. LINDA S CATRON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion For Ordefr Of Contempt
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, September 14, 2012, Line 1, PLAINTIFF YIN FALK Ntc Of Motion For Order Of Contempt Pro Tem Judge Rebecca Woodson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Judgment Creditor's Motion for Order of Contempt is GRANTED. Judgment Creditor's Motion to Compel Responses to Post-Judgment Discovery Requests and Production of Documents is GRANTED. Judgment Creditor's Motion for Further Monetary Sanctions in the amount of $2,190.00 is GRANTED. Judgment Creditor's Request for Additional Sanctions of $2,000.00 for contempt is DENIED. Moving party to submit a proposed Order at the time and place of hearing. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rwoodson@mckennalong.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: FRANK L. VINTEAN et al VS. WASHINGTON SQUARE BAR & GRILL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, September 14, 2012, Line 4, DEFENDANTS COBALT, LLC, GUY FERRI, KEN YOUNG'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Denied in its entirety. Moving Parties did not satisfy their burden of production. There is a triable issue whether Moving Parties owed a duty to Plaintiff with regards to the stairway. Moving Parties failed to present affirmative evidence showing, as a matter of law, Plaintiff's claim cannot be established. The Court will not consider Moving Parties' arguments to the specific causes of actions in Cross Complainant Callan's cross complaint as those are not properly before the Court. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: LENA A. AJAMIAN VS. CANTORCO2E, L.P., A PARTNERSHIP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION And Notice Of Motion To Compel Depositions Of Joshua Margolis, Patrick Pfeiffer, And Donald Allan; Memo Of Points
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, September 14, 2012, Line 2, PLAINTIFF LENA AJAMIAN MOTION And Notice Of Motion To Compel Depositions Of Joshua Margolis, Patrick Pfeiffer, And Donald Allan Pro Tem Judge Rebecca Woodson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff's Motion to Compel Depositions is GRANTED IN PART. The parties are ordered to appear and inform the Court of dates certain for the depositions of J. Margolis, P. Pfeiffer, D. Allan and plaintiff L. Ajamian, which shall be set for a date no later than October 26, 2012. Moving party to prepare an Order to issue at the time and place of hearing. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rwoodson@mckennalong.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL E. BUTLER VS. LAUREEN PARAGUYA DR et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, September 14, 2012, Line 12, DEFENDANTS ALLAN PONT, M.D., SUTTER HEALTH, SUTTER WEST BAY HOSPITALS' DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT. Sustained without leave to amend as to all claims. The causes of action alleged in the Third Amended Complaint arise from medical negligence and thus the one-year statute of limitations for medical malpractice cases under Code of Civil Procedure 340.5 applies. The alleged injury was discovered in November 2008 and the Complaint was not filed until June 29, 2011. Accordingly, the claims are time-barred. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: SOPHIANE BENAOUDA VS. KHRG MONACO SF, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Reconsideration Of This Court'S Order To Compel Arbitration And Stay The Case
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, September 14, 2012, Line 16, PLAINTIFF SOPHIANE BENAOUDA'S Motion For Reconsideration Of This Court's Order To Compel Arbitration And Stay The Case. The motion for reconsideration is granted and, upon reconsideration, the petition to compel arbitration is denied. The agreement to arbitrate, which was subject to the employee handbook, is unconscionable.
